summary: Compared the full-memory improvement plan against current DinoBrain status, implemented the next gap-closing slice for task lifecycle settlement, applied safe auto-close settlement for 6 eligible stale hook/proof tasks, regenerated status/RAG/review/semantic/index reports, verified the app, committed the app changes as 21940da, and pushed main to origin.
decisions: Treat the document comparison as an implementation backlog, not only a report: status freshness and full-memory audit were already present, so the next missing operational gate was lifecycle settlement.
Added a deterministic task lifecycle settlement gate to reduce stale active task noise without touching manual repair cases.
Applied settlement only to auto-close-safe candidates; manual stale review and trace reconstruction remain intentionally untouched.
Did not push the broad dinobrain-data dirty state because the data repo contains hundreds of generated/untracked candidate/review/log artifacts outside the scoped app change.
next_steps: Repair the remaining 6 manual lifecycle blockers: 5 manual_stale_review_required and 1 manual_trace_reconstruction_required.
Implement real dense retrieval or embedding-backed hybrid retrieval so RAG eval no longer reports lexical fallback only.
Replace fallback golden RAG cases with user/domain golden cases and turn memory-on versus memory-off quality into a CI/regular gate.
Create a narrow data-sync settlement policy before pushing the broad dinobrain-data backlog.
